summary: Tested PropFlo CRM (FastAPI + Expo Router) end-to-end. Backend: 16/16 pytest passed including auth, dashboard stats, seeded lists, leads CRUD, AI summary, deals stage update, task complete. Frontend: login (prefilled creds), dashboard KPIs, all 5 bottom tabs, leads filter chips, lead detail, new-lead screen, property cards + detail, pipeline kanban with 6 columns and move-stage sheet, More -> Tasks/Contacts navigation all work cleanly. Two issues found (see below).

minor.issue: Returned 500 on second call during UI test. Backend log shows 'litellm.BadRequestError: Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.001175, Max budget: 0.001' from EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. The pytest call (first request) succeeded. This is NOT a code bug; it is the LLM key/budget configuration. Backend correctly surfaces the error as 500.

ui_bugs.issue: Logout uses React Native Alert.alert which on web maps to window.confirm. It works for real users (one click on OK) but is a deviation from the otherwise custom dark UI and is slightly fragile for automated tests. Suggest using a custom themed modal for consistency on web.

issues: RN-Web warning: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use 'boxShadow'. Cosmetic console warning only, no functional impact.

action_items: Verify EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget for /api/leads/{id}/ai-summary - the current key reports max budget 0.001 and is exceeded after one call. Either rotate / top up the key or expect the AI Summary feature to fail on subsequent calls.
(Optional) Replace Alert.alert with a custom themed confirm modal in (tabs)/more.tsx logout flow to match dark/orange theme on web.
(Optional) Migrate React Native shadow* props to boxShadow to silence deprecation warnings on web.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False is fine since frontend uses Bearer token in AsyncStorage; cookies are still set but not required for the mobile flow.
bcrypt hashing ($2b$) with JWT HS256, 7-day expiry - matches playbook expectations.
Brute-force lockout implemented (5 attempts -> 15 min) - confirmed in code (not exhaustively tested to avoid locking admin).
seed_users is idempotent (checks find_one before insert). seed_demo_data is gated on leads count == 0 - good.
server.py is 663 lines, approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting routers (auth/leads/properties/etc.) into modules in next iteration.

success_rate.backend: 100% (16/16 pytest); AI summary fails after LLM key budget exhausted (infra, not code)
success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all major flows verified; logout depends on browser confirm dialog)

seed_data_creation: Used backend's built-in seeding (admin@propflo.com, agent@propflo.com, 6 leads, 6 properties, 4 contacts, 6 deals, 5 tasks). No manual seeding required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL is in EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (not EXPO_BACKEND_URL): https://realty-connect-113.preview.emergentagent.com. Bottom-tab labels (Leads/Properties/Pipeline/More) are not unique on the page; use page.get_by_role('button', name=label, exact=True).last to navigate. AI summary endpoint may return 500 if the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is exhausted - check /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for litellm BadRequestError before assuming a code bug.
rca_of_issue: AI Summary 500: emergentintegrations -> litellm -> OpenAIException 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.001175, Max budget: 0.001'. Reproduction: call POST /api/leads/{id}/ai-summary twice in quick succession with the current EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Mitigation: rotate/refresh the key or raise the budget; backend code correctly catches and returns 500 with detail message.
